tracks.name: Coding
tracks.headline: From visual blocks to Python. Ages 5–12+.
tracks.subtitle: 7 programs that take kids from their first drag-and-drop to writing real code.

programs.name: First Steps in Coding (ScratchJr & Tynker)
programs.description: Visual block-based coding for the youngest coders. Kids create animations, stories, and simple games — the perfect first step into the world of programming.

programs.name: Coding and AI in Minecraft
programs.description: Kids learn coding by building and modding in the world they already love. Introduces AI concepts naturally through Minecraft's environment.
programs.equipment: Laptop with Minecraft (Education or Java edition)

programs.name: Create 3D Worlds in Delightrex
programs.description: 3D world-building and game design. Kids create immersive environments while learning intermediate programming logic and spatial thinking.

programs.name: Build & Code in Roblox
programs.description: Game development on Roblox using Lua scripting. Kids build, publish, and share their own multiplayer games with real players.

programs.name: Python Coding
programs.description: Text-based programming with Python — the language used at Google, NASA, and Netflix. Real projects, real problem-solving, real skills.

programs.name: Unity 3D Game Studio
programs.description: Professional game development using the Unity engine and C#. Kids create 3D games from scratch — the same tools used by real studios.

programs.name: 3D Modeling
programs.description: Digital 3D design and modeling. Skills applicable to gaming, architecture, product design, and animation — a creative-technical hybrid.

tracks.name: Robotics
tracks.headline: From screen-free robots to Arduino circuits. Ages 4–12+.
tracks.subtitle: 7 programs that take kids from their first robot to real electronics and physics.

programs.name: First Robotics with Qobo
programs.description: Screen-free robotics for the youngest learners. Hands-on play with Qobo robots introduces sequencing, logic, and cause-and-effect — no screens needed.

programs.name: Smart Robotics for Kids
programs.description: Building and programming simple robots. Sensors, motors, and basic logic — everything is hands-on and tactile.

programs.name: Mechanical Robotics Lab
programs.description: Engineering meets coding. Kids build mechanical systems — gears, levers, motors — then program their creations to move and respond.

programs.name: Essential Robotics Skills
programs.description: Core robotics competencies: sensor integration, autonomous movement, and complex problem-solving through building and coding.

programs.name: Robotics Prime
programs.description: Advanced robotics with complex builds and sophisticated programming. Prepares kids for competitions and real-world engineering challenges.

programs.name: Physics in Robotics
programs.description: Applies physics principles — force, motion, energy — through robotics projects. Science, engineering, and coding in one program.

programs.name: Arduino Robotics & Circuits
programs.description: Real electronics: Arduino microcontrollers, circuits, sensors, actuators. The bridge from educational robotics to real engineering.

tracks.name: Creativity
tracks.headline: Digital art from finger painting to professional illustration. Ages 4–11+.
tracks.subtitle: Creative-technical programs that bridge art and technology.

programs.name: Digital Art for Kids
programs.description: Introduction to digital drawing and creative expression. Kids explore color, shape, and composition using kid-friendly digital tools.

programs.name: Digital Drawing Pro
programs.description: Intermediate digital art: techniques, composition, and digital painting. Kids develop a personal style while learning professional techniques.

programs.name: Digital Illustration with Procreate
programs.description: Professional-level digital illustration using Procreate. Portfolio-ready skills that open doors to design, animation, and art careers.
